title: senna glutinosa subsp. charlesiana
intro: Senna glutinosa subsp. charlesiana, also known as Charles's senna, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the legume family that is native to Southeast Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its laxative effects due to the presence of anthraquinone glycosides. The health benefits of Charles's senna include relief from constipation, as well as potential uses as a diuretic and to reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and pods of the plant can be dried and used to make teas, capsules, or infusions for medicinal purposes.
